pattern: A two-channel dynamic program over the grid carries both the best reachable score and how many ways achieve it, combining predecessors by taking the larger score and summing the ways that tie it.
hints: Process cells from the start corner toward the end corner so that each cell's three possible predecessors (the moves up, left, and up-left) are already solved.
Keep two tables per cell: the maximum score to reach it and the number of distinct paths that attain that maximum.

When merging predecessors, take the highest predecessor score; among predecessors tied at that score, add their path counts (modulo 1e9+7), then add the cell's own digit. Obstacles and the start and end cells contribute no digit.

edge_inputs.description: two paths tie for the maximum
smallest grid with a direct diagonal
obstacle right next to the start blocks one direction
open grid with many equal-score paths

stress.description: moderately sized grid with sparse obstacles to exercise the two-channel dp
stress.note: fixed E and S corners; modest obstacle density keeps paths plentiful
